steps.title: Identify the structural relationship
steps.content:
The main clause 'divergent strategies for preserving milk [blank]' needs an elaboration: the sentence then explains how these strategies diverged by contrasting two approaches (rennet-coagulation in Europe/Southwest Asia versus acid-heat coagulation in the steppe).

The 'whereas' clause is not just a contrast—it's an elaboration that defines what 'divergent strategies' means.

steps.title: Apply the colon rule for elaboration
steps.content:
A colon is used to introduce an independent clause or clause structure that elaborates on or explains a preceding statement.

Here, the 'whereas' clause (which presents both types of divergent strategies) elaborates on what 'divergent strategies emerged' means.

The colon correctly signals this elaborating relationship. Options A and D fail to mark this as elaboration; option B ('emerged and') is nonsensical with 'whereas'; the comma in D is too weak for introducing a complex elaborating structure with multiple coordinate examples. That matches choice C — C is correct.
